commands.summary: Auto-update decision helper: only runs in a packaged build and honours FORGELINK_DISABLE_UPDATES=1 opt-out.
Diagnostics now reports app_version (FORGELINK_APP_VERSION=9.9.9 asserted) alongside the existing redaction checks.

commands.command: npx electron-builder --dir; npx asar list ...; launch ForgeLink.exe
commands.summary: packaging.test.js caught that main.js required ./updates without updates.js being in builder.json files (the missing-module crash class from PR-013); after adding it, updates.js is bundled and the app launches fully (5 processes). electron-updater itself is not yet in the asar under the current files allowlist, so the guarded auto-update path stays inert in installed builds (documented in the release checklist).

commands.command: npm run scan:secrets && npm run scan:deps
commands.summary: Repo secret scan clean (CHANGELOG/docs added no secrets); production dependency audit reports 0 vulnerabilities. A dev-only undici advisory introduced by electron-updater's dev tree is out of the shipped tree.
Full suite green (live Twilio skipped); validate_repo + repopact doctor healthy.

environment.process_note: PR-014 unblocked items delivered: version metadata (status + diagnostics app_version), release notes (CHANGELOG.md), a reproducible release checklist (docs/release-checklist.md), and auto-update WIRING (tested helper + guarded main-process check + GitHub publish config + electron-updater dependency). PR-014 stays PENDING: it needs a code-signing certificate (operator-provided), electron-updater bundled into the asar, and a published release feed before auto-update is functional and trust-anchored. These remaining steps are enumerated in the release checklist.
